text: I find myself in an environment shaped by glass, technology, and anticipation—the first space quiet and sterile, almost meditative in its emptiness, inviting contemplation about boundaries and transparency, both literal and figurative. Entering the next, I am confronted by vibrant colors and bold ideas, immersed in the future-facing ethos of research and development. The language of curiosity and innovation surrounds me, evoking excitement and a quiet awe. I'm aware of humanity's ongoing desire not only to understand the world but to reshape it, and I feel both exhilarated and slightly humbled to stand at this intersection of possibility and reflection.
